differences: Purchase rows retain faint up/down reorder controls on the left edge; the fixed prototype has no visible reorder control.
The mobile action buttons and share icon are close to the prototype but not pixel-identical in glyph shape and width.
Read-only completed detail uses MVP copy for the immutable-state notice because completed-list behavior remains MVP-governed.

classified_differences.difference: Visible reorder controls on purchase rows
classified_differences.reason: Slice 11 reorder behavior is an existing MVP mobile interaction and is covered by E2E tests.
Read-only completed shopping semantics and user feedback remain MVP-governed per the responsibility matrix.

suggestions: If product approves hiding mobile reorder controls, remove the faint arrow overlay and update Slice 11 expectations.
During the pantry-reflect picker batch, keep the shopping detail shell untouched and port only the popup/sheet component.
reasoning: The primary mobile shell, summary band, grouped item cards, CTA, and bottom tab match the fixed shopping-detail reference closely. Remaining differences are classified as MVP functional preservation rather than unclassified visual drift.
